title: ATER Stock Q1 2022 Earnings Expectations
text: Earnings Announcement: May 9, 2022. Two Key Bearish Factors: 1) Inconsistent Earnings History: Aterian has a history of missing earnings per share (EPS) estimates by a wide margin. In the five quarters preceding this analysis, the company achieved only one EPS beat against four misses. The Q1 2022 EPS estimate stood at negative 26 cents. 2) Declining Profit Margins: In 2021 results, the company reported that the contribution margin declined to 10.1% from 13.5% in 2020, reflecting impacts from global supply chain disruptions and related inflation. The analyst suggests further margin compression is likely given ongoing supply chain challenges and elevated inflation. Despite 33.4% revenue growth to $247.8 million in 2021, Aterian reported widening losses and has been unprofitable since 2017. Share dilution increased substantially, with outstanding shares growing 120.3% over the past year. Analyst Recommendation: Avoid the stock, citing insufficient financial strength to support sustained gains.
